concerns.text: The plan relies on a 'synthetic TSI data' fallback (Option B) because the verified TSI URL is missing/mismatched. This violates Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy) and Principle I (Reproducibility) as the core research output cannot be reproduced from canonical sources if the primary dataset is invalid. The plan must halt or explicitly define a verified TSI source before proceeding.

The plan claims 'Compliant' for Principle II (Verified Accuracy) despite the research.md explicitly stating 'NO VERIFIED URL FOUND' for TSI and relying on synthetic generation. This is a direct contradiction; the plan cannot be compliant if the data source is unverified.

Phase 3 applies the model to pre-satellite data (1610–2002) but the training data (if synthetic) does not represent the physical reality of the satellite era. Training on synthetic data and applying to historical data violates the scientific method for this research question, as the model learns a fabricated relationship rather than the actual sunspot-TSI correlation.

Principle VI (Cycle-Specific Calibration) is marked 'Compliant' with the note 'Plan explicitly includes Cycle ID...'. However, if the training data is synthetic (as per research.md), the 'Cycle ID' feature may not capture real cycle-to-cycle variability, rendering the compliance claim misleading regarding the actual scientific validity.
